name: Hen & Chickens
region: North America, Usa, Florida, Florida Keys, Islamorada
description: This shallow reef in the Florida Keys is perfect for beginners and snorkelers. Vibrant coral formations teem with tropical fish, including parrotfish, angelfish, and occasional nurse sharks. The site's scattered coral heads resemble a hen and her chicks, creating interesting swim-throughs. Clear waters and gentle currents make for relaxed diving. Expect excellent visibility and abundant marine life in this easy-access Atlantic dive spot.

name: Delta Shoal
region: North America, Usa, Florida, Florida Keys, Marathon
description: Delta Shoal sits in the Florida Keys' clear waters, offering a mix of coral formations and sandy patches. The site teems with tropical fish, stingrays, and occasional reef sharks. Strong currents attract pelagics, making drift dives exciting. Depths range from 15 to 40 feet, ideal for all skill levels. The coral-covered limestone ledges create swim-throughs and overhangs to explore. Visibility often exceeds 60 feet, with warm water year-round. A great spot for both macro life and larger marine encounters.

name: Pickles Reef
region: North America, Usa, Florida, Florida Keys, Islamorada
description: Pickles Reef sits in the Florida Keys near Islamorada, offering shallow coral formations perfect for snorkelers and divers. The site features scattered elkhorn and star corals, along with vibrant mix of tropical fish, lobster, and occasional sea turtles. Clear waters and gentle currents make it ideal for beginners. The reef structure creates swim-throughs and small ledges to explore. Depths range from 10 to 20 feet, with excellent visibility most days. Nearby spur-and-groove formations add variety to the dive.

name: American Shoal
region: North America, Usa, Florida, Florida Keys, Sugarloaf Key
description: American Shoal sits off Sugarloaf Key in the Florida Keys, where the Atlantic Ocean meets the Straits of Florida. The site features a historic lighthouse and vibrant coral formations teeming with tropical fish, barracuda, and occasional reef sharks. Strong currents attract pelagic species, making it ideal for drift diving. The shallow reef slopes to deeper ledges, offering diverse depths for all skill levels. Visibility is typically excellent, with warm waters year-round. A great spot for both marine life encounters and underwater photography.

name: Duane
region: North America, Usa, Florida, Florida Keys, Islamorada
description: The Duane wreck sits upright in 120 feet of clear Atlantic water off Islamorada. This former Coast Guard cutter is encrusted with colorful corals and sponges, attracting schools of barracuda, grouper, and the occasional reef shark. Strong currents make this advanced dive site ideal for drift diving along the intact superstructure. Penetration opportunities exist for certified wreck divers, with swim-throughs revealing the ship's history. Visibility often exceeds 100 feet, showcasing the marine life that thrives on this artificial reef.

name: Bache Shoal
region: North America, Usa, Florida, Florida Keys, Islandia
description: This shallow reef in the Florida Keys features vibrant coral formations and abundant marine life. The site offers easy diving with depths under 30 feet, perfect for beginners. Expect schools of tropical fish, occasional rays, and lobster sightings. The sandy patches between coral heads attract garden eels. Strong currents sometimes sweep through, bringing pelagic visitors. Visibility typically ranges 40-60 feet. The nearby Gulf Stream influences water conditions.
